data_sources: Georgia Department of Public Health EMS Division (dph.georgia.gov/EMS)
FY 2021 GTCNC EMS Trauma Related Equipment Grant - Ambulance Count by County (trauma.georgia.gov)
Priority Ambulance / Central EMS / National EMS / Puckett EMS / Ambucare (priorityambulance.com)
Georgia Emergency Management & Homeland Security Agency 911 Center Directory (gema.georgia.gov)
notes: Georgia has 159 counties. EMS is organized into 10 EMS regions. Providers include county-run third service, fire-based, hospital-based, and private contracted services. Grady EMS (hospital-based out of Atlanta) has aggressively expanded across the state, especially in rural south Georgia. Priority Ambulance operates four brands in GA: Central EMS, National EMS, Puckett EMS, and Ambucare. AMR holds contracts in several metro-area counties. Many rural counties operate their own county EMS as a third service.

agencies.name: Grady EMS
agencies.notes: Largest ambulance provider in Georgia. Serves City of Atlanta and south Fulton County for 911, plus 16+ rural counties mostly in south Georgia. Handles ~140,000 requests annually. South Georgia HQ in Albany. Also provides specialty care transports statewide.

agencies.name: American Medical Response (AMR)
agencies.notes: AMR serves North Fulton County (separate zone from Grady's south Fulton/Atlanta zone). DeKalb County contract extended through June 2026 with $4.89M subsidy. Troup County 911 provider since 1993. AMR Coweta also covers Meriwether and Fayette counties and South Fulton areas.

agencies.name: National EMS (Priority Ambulance)
agencies.notes: Founded 1976. Georgia's first nationally accredited (CAAS) EMS provider (1983). Acquired by Priority Ambulance in 2018. Serves Athens-Clarke County, Oconee, Morgan, Rockdale. Also provides 911 in Newton County zone (Piedmont Newton Hospital partnership). UGA Athletic Association preferred provider.

agencies.name: Central EMS (Priority Ambulance)
agencies.notes: Exclusive 911 provider for Forsyth County since 2015. Took over Richmond County (Augusta) in April 2023 after Gold Cross EMS exited. Also 911 provider in Newton County. Provides transport services in Bibb, Bulloch, Chatham, Coweta, Fulton areas.

agencies.name: Puckett EMS (Priority Ambulance)
agencies.notes: 911 provider for southwest Cobb County (Powder Springs, Mableton, Austell area, ~350K population). Previously held Walker and Dade county contracts (transitioned to CHI Memorial EMS in 2021 for those counties). Was Catoosa County provider before Angel EMS.

agencies.name: Ambucare (Priority Ambulance)
agencies.notes: Primary 911 provider for Haralson County since 1973. Joined Priority Ambulance in 2021. CAAS accredited. Mutual aid with Polk, Paulding, and Carroll counties. Also provides non-emergency transport to surrounding counties.

agencies.name: MetroAtlanta Ambulance Service (MAAS)
agencies.notes: Founded 2001. Designated 911 ambulance provider for Bartow, Cobb, and Paulding counties. Covers 1,200 square miles and 1M+ residents. Cobb County uses a tiered model: fire departments provide first response ALS, MAAS handles ambulance transport. Also operates as Coastal EMS in greater Savannah area.

agencies.name: Community Ambulance (MGAS Holdings)
agencies.notes: Formerly Mid Georgia Ambulance. CAAS accredited. 250+ EMS professionals. Serves 5 Georgia counties with 911 coverage. In Bibb County shares 911 zones with Atrium Health Navicent EMS. Also covers Lamar, Upson, Crawford, and Muscogee counties.

agencies.name: Gold Cross EMS
agencies.notes: CAAS accredited. 911 provider for Columbia and Jefferson counties. Previously served Richmond County but exited in April 2023 (Central EMS took over). Partnered with SouthStar EMS in 2021.

agencies.name: AmeriPro EMS (AmeriPro Health)
agencies.notes: Provides 911, BLS/ALS, CCT, and interfacility ambulance services. Specializes in rural market EMS. Partnership with Upson and Lamar counties announced 2022. Some overlap with Grady EMS in certain counties.

agencies.name: West Georgia Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: 911 provider for Carroll County for 50+ years. Acquired by Tanner Health System. Holds the 911 EMS Zone License for Carroll County.

agencies.name: Chatham Emergency Services (CES)
agencies.notes: Countywide exclusive 911 emergency and non-emergency ambulance service. Community-owned nonprofit since 1961. 630 sq miles, 320K population. 75,000+ EMS calls/year. 250 employees. CAAS accredited. One of only 7 CAAS-accredited services in Georgia.

agencies.name: Gwinnett County Fire & Emergency Services
agencies.notes: Serves 1M+ population. 31 fire stations, 33 ambulances. 107,000+ requests annually. Transport fee $975 flat + $15/mile. All vehicles ALS equipped.

agencies.name: Clayton County Fire & Emergency Services
agencies.notes: 12 ALS ambulances, 3 Community Treatment units with NPs and paramedics, 1 Community Care Unit. City of Forest Park Fire EMS (4 ambulances) and City of Morrow Fire/EMS (3 ambulances) also serve within Clayton County.

agencies.name: Douglas County Fire/EMS
agencies.notes: Fire department assumed EMS coverage, changed name to Douglas County Fire/EMS.

agencies.name: Fayette County Fire & Emergency Services
agencies.notes: Primary fire, EMS, and EMA organization for Fayette County. 7 ALS ambulances staffed with paramedic and AEMT minimum. Peachtree City Fire Department also operates 7 ambulances within Fayette County.

agencies.name: Henry County Fire Department
agencies.notes: CAAHEP accredited Paramedic Program. Fire-based EMS transport.

agencies.name: Cherokee County Fire & Emergency Services
agencies.notes: Internationally accredited, ISO Class 2 rated. 28 stations covering 434 sq miles, ~300K residents.

agencies.name: Atrium Health Floyd EMS
agencies.notes: Hospital-based 911 EMS. Designated provider for Floyd and Chattooga counties (also Cherokee County, Alabama). Only Level II Trauma Center in NW Georgia. Four-time Georgia EMS of the Year. Provides field blood transfusions.

agencies.name: Redmond Regional EMS
agencies.notes: Hospital-based EMS serving Chattooga County (4 ambulances) and Polk County (6 ambulances). Chattooga County has both Floyd EMS and Redmond Regional operating.

agencies.name: Hamilton Emergency Medical Services (HEMS)
agencies.notes: Exclusive 911 coverage for Whitfield County (~104K residents) under performance contract. Designated Advanced Life Support service. 2019 Region 1 EMS of the Year.

agencies.name: Angel EMS (Priority Ambulance)
agencies.notes: 911 provider for Catoosa County since 2009. Joined Priority Ambulance in 2018. 14 ambulances, 60+ employees. Provides non-emergency to Walker, Dade, Whitfield.

agencies.name: CHI Memorial EMS
agencies.notes: Took over Walker and Dade county 911 contracts from Puckett EMS in February 2021. 32+ years of EMS experience. 5 ambulances for 24-hour service. Received $1.3M grants for equipment upgrades in 2025.

agencies.name: Dougherty County EMS
agencies.notes: Career EMS system in Albany, GA. Sole 911 provider for Dougherty County. 22,000+ EMS calls/year. Six 24/7 ALS ambulances + one 12-hour unit. 52 career paramedics/AEMTs + 25 part-time. 5 stations. 334 sq mile area, ~89,500 population.

agencies.name: SGMC Health EMS (South Georgia Medical Center)
agencies.notes: Hospital-based ambulance service. 73 medics, 10 ambulances. Responds within 1,200 sq mile region. ~1,508 calls/month. 40+ years of service. Region 8 EMS Service of the Year.

agencies.name: Atrium Health Navicent EMS
agencies.notes: Formerly Medical Center of Central Georgia. Serves central Georgia across six counties. In Bibb County shares 911 zones with Community Ambulance. Also known as The Medical Center of Central Georgia EMS.

agencies.name: Heartland EMS
agencies.notes: Serves rural central Georgia counties. Listed in GTCNC grant for Bleckley (12 ambulances), Pulaski (1), Wilkinson (2).

agencies.name: Emory Healthcare EMS (Houston County EMS)
agencies.notes: Provides 24/7 911 emergency transportation to Houston County. 33,000+ ER responses/year. 70+ paramedics and EMTs. 18 ambulances, 8 stations. Formerly Houston Healthcare EMS.

agencies.name: Columbus Fire & Emergency Medical Services
agencies.notes: Serves Columbus/Muscogee consolidated government. Shares 911 coverage with EMS Care Ambulance and Community Ambulance.

agencies.name: EMS Care Ambulance
agencies.notes: 911 contract with Columbus/Muscogee County. Also serves Chattahoochee County. Provides emergency, long-distance, and facility transfers.

agencies.name: Gold Star EMS
agencies.notes: Listed in GTCNC grant for Sumter County (5 ambulances) and Clinch County (2 ambulances).

agencies.name: Stephens County EMS
agencies.notes: Joint agreement between Stephens County Board of Commissioners and Hospital Authority. Station One on hospital campus. ALS units handle 911 and critical care transfers.

agencies.name: Emanuel County EMS
agencies.notes: Joint venture between Emanuel Medical Center (operates service, employs personnel) and County of Emanuel (funds payroll, maintains fleet).

agencies.name: Coffee Regional Medical Center EMS
agencies.notes: Hospital-based EMS serving Coffee County.

agencies.name: LifeBrite EMS
agencies.notes: Listed as LifeBrite of Early County in GTCNC grant.

agencies.name: WellStar Spalding EMS
agencies.notes: Listed as Spalding Regional Medical Center EMS. Joint facility with Griffin-Spalding County E9-1-1.

agencies.name: Northeast Georgia Physicians Group EMS
agencies.notes: Public-private partnership with Barrow County. Operates ambulances from 6 fire stations + 1 at NGMC Barrow. Also serves White County. Hospital system-affiliated EMS.

agencies.name: Mercy Ambulance (Chatham)
agencies.notes: Listed in GTCNC grant as Chatham-Mercy Ambulance with 46 ambulances. Likely operates alongside Chatham Emergency Services.

agencies.name: Excelsior Ambulance
agencies.notes: Listed in GTCNC grant for Long County with 6 ambulances.

agencies.name: Toombs-Montgomery EMS
agencies.notes: Joint county EMS service covering both Toombs and Montgomery counties. 8 ambulances in Toombs, 1 in Montgomery.

agencies.name: Veterans Medical Transport
agencies.notes: Listed as Pike-Veterans Medical Transport in GTCNC grant.

agencies.name: Taylor Regional Hospital EMS
agencies.notes: Hospital-based EMS listed under Pulaski County in GTCNC grant.

agencies.name: Atlanta Fire Rescue Department
agencies.notes: Provides first response within City of Atlanta. Grady EMS handles primary ambulance transport. Atlanta Fire operates 5 ambulances as supplemental.

agencies.name: City of Hapeville EMS
agencies.notes: Hapeville operates its own ambulance service within Fulton County. Small city south of Atlanta near airport.

agencies.name: Piedmont Newton Hospital EMS
agencies.notes: Hospital-based EMS. Partners with Priority Ambulance/National EMS for 911 response in Newton County.

agencies.name: Colquitt/Miller County Fire/EMS
agencies.notes: Combined EMS service for Colquitt and Miller counties. Colquitt County EMS has 7 ambulances, Miller has 4.

agencies.name: Jackson County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. 7 ALS ambulances (6 staffed 24/7, 1 for 12 hours). 95%+ staff are paramedics. Located in Athens area.

agencies.name: Walton County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. 6 ambulances 24/7 in strategic locations + 2 reserve. Staffed by EMT-Is and Paramedics.

agencies.name: Barrow County Fire and EMS
agencies.notes: Fire-based EMS with public-private partnership through Northeast Georgia Physicians Group. 6 fire stations with 8 ambulances + 1 at NGMC Barrow.

agencies.name: Madison County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS in the Athens area.

agencies.name: Oglethorpe County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS in the Athens area.

agencies.name: Greene County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. 3 stations strategically placed. Most of population within 10-minute response. Athens area.

agencies.name: Elbert County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS in Region 10.

agencies.name: Thomas County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. 7 ALS ambulances + 1 extrication unit. ~10,100 calls/year. Serves Thomasville, Barwick, Boston, Coolidge, Meigs, Metcalf, Ochlocknee, Pavo.

agencies.name: Tift County Fire and Rescue (EMS)
agencies.notes: EMS is part of Tift County Department of Public Safety along with Fire Rescue, E-911, and Emergency Management.

agencies.name: Harris County EMS
agencies.notes: ALS ambulance service county-wide. 5 EMS stations, 24/7 with 2 paramedics/EMTs per shift per station.

agencies.name: Glynn County Fire Department EMS
agencies.notes: Combined fire and ambulance service. Station 8 provides EMS. Jekyll Island Fire/EMS operates 2 additional ambulances within Glynn County.

agencies.name: Habersham County Emergency Services
agencies.notes: ~100 full/part-time personnel. 50,000+ citizens served. 6 firehouses + 1 EMS house. 12,000+ calls/year. 5 24/7 paramedic-staffed med units.

agencies.name: Hall County Fire Services EMS
agencies.notes: Fire-based EMS. EMS Division at 470 Crescent Drive, Gainesville. Northeast Georgia Medical Center is the major hospital in the county.

agencies.name: Dawson County Emergency Services
agencies.notes: Operates 4 paramedic-level ambulances + 1 paramedic-level engine daily.

agencies.name: Bryan County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS in coastal Georgia, Region 9.

agencies.name: Bulloch County EMS
agencies.notes: Advanced Life Support ambulance service serving Bulloch County (Statesboro area).

agencies.name: Effingham County EMS
agencies.notes: County EMS in coastal Georgia, Region 9.

agencies.name: Liberty Regional EMS
agencies.notes: Full-time EMS covering all of Liberty County. Region 9.

agencies.name: Camden County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 9 coastal Georgia.

agencies.name: Ware County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Waycross area. Region 9.

agencies.name: Laurens County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Fleet of 10 ALS ambulances. 24/7/365 emergency medical response.

agencies.name: Crisp County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 8 south Georgia.

agencies.name: Lee County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 8 south Georgia.

agencies.name: Burke County EMA/EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 6 east Georgia.

agencies.name: McDuffie County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 6 east Georgia.

agencies.name: Gordon County Ambulance
agencies.notes: County-run ambulance service. Region 1 northwest Georgia.

agencies.name: Murray EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 1 northwest Georgia.

agencies.name: Pickens County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 1 northwest Georgia.

agencies.name: Fannin County Fire and EMS
agencies.notes: Combined fire/EMS. Region 1 northwest Georgia mountains.

agencies.name: Gilmer County Fire and EMS
agencies.notes: Combined fire/EMS. Region 1 northwest Georgia mountains.

agencies.name: Banks County Fire and EMS
agencies.notes: Combined fire/EMS. Region 2 northeast Georgia.

agencies.name: Franklin County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 2 northeast Georgia.

agencies.name: Hart County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 2 northeast Georgia.

agencies.name: Lumpkin County Emergency Services
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 2 northeast Georgia. Dahlonega area.

agencies.name: Rabun County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 2 northeast Georgia mountains.

agencies.name: Towns County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 2 northeast Georgia mountains.

agencies.name: Union County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 2 northeast Georgia mountains. Blairsville area.

agencies.name: DeKalb County Fire
agencies.notes: DeKalb County Fire operates 7 ambulances as supplemental to AMR which holds the primary 911 ambulance contract (48 ambulances).

agencies.name: Coweta County Fire/Rescue EMS
agencies.notes: County fire provides first response. AMR Coweta provides ambulance transport. 15 ambulances listed in GTCNC grant under Coweta County EMS.

agencies.name: Meriwether County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. AMR Coweta also provides transport. Region 4.

agencies.name: Butts County Fire Department EMS
agencies.notes: Fire-based EMS. Region 4.

agencies.name: Heard County EMA/EMS
agencies.notes: County emergency management/EMS. Region 4.

agencies.name: Grady County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS (not to be confused with Grady EMS/Grady Health System). Grady County is in southwest Georgia near Thomasville. Region 8.

agencies.name: Berrien County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 8 south Georgia.

agencies.name: Calhoun County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 8 south Georgia.

agencies.name: Dooly County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 8 south Georgia.

agencies.name: Irwin County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 8 south Georgia.

agencies.name: Terrell County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 8 south Georgia.

agencies.name: Turner County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 8 south Georgia.

agencies.name: Dodge County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 5 central Georgia.

agencies.name: Jasper County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 5 central Georgia.

agencies.name: Johnson County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 5 central Georgia.

agencies.name: Monroe County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 5 central Georgia.

agencies.name: Peach County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Fort Valley area. Region 5.

agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Eatonton area. Region 5.

agencies.name: Telfair County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 5 central Georgia.

agencies.name: Washington County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Sandersville area. Region 5.

agencies.name: Wheeler County Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: County-run ambulance service. Region 5.

agencies.name: Wilcox County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 5.

agencies.name: Wilkinson County Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: County-run ambulance service. Heartland EMS also operates 2 ambulances in Wilkinson. Region 5.

agencies.name: Jenkins County Ambulance Service
agencies.notes: County-run ambulance service. Region 6.

agencies.name: Lincoln County OES/EMS
agencies.notes: Office of Emergency Services provides EMS. Region 6.

agencies.name: Screven County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 6 east Georgia.

agencies.name: Warren County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 6.

agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 6 east Georgia.

agencies.name: Macon County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS (not to be confused with Macon-Bibb County). Region 7 west central Georgia.

agencies.name: Marion County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 7.

agencies.name: Schley County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 7.

agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 7.

agencies.name: Webster County Fire/EMS
agencies.notes: Combined fire/EMS. Region 7. One of Georgia's smallest counties.

agencies.name: Appling County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 9 southeast Georgia.

agencies.name: Atkinson County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 9.

agencies.name: Alma Bacon County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Alma is the county seat. Region 9.

agencies.name: Brantley County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Region 9 southeast Georgia.

agencies.name: Candler County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS. Metter area. Region 9.

agencies.name: Charlton County EMS

agencies.name: Wayne County EMS

agencies.name: Bartow County EMS / Bartow County Fire
agencies.notes: Bartow County EMS operates 11 ambulances (MetroAtlanta Ambulance contract). Bartow County Fire operates 2 additional. MAAS is the designated 911 ambulance transport provider.

agencies.name: Dade County EMS
agencies.notes: County-run EMS operates 5 ambulances. CHI Memorial Hospital EMS also operates 2 ambulances in Dade County. CHI Memorial took over primary 911 contract in 2021.

agencies.name: West Point Fire Department EMS
agencies.notes: City of West Point operates 2 ambulances within Troup County. AMR is the primary county 911 provider.

agencies.name: Peachtree City Fire Department EMS
agencies.notes: City of Peachtree City operates 7 ambulances within Fayette County alongside Fayette County Fire & Emergency Services.

agencies.name: City of Forest Park Fire EMS
agencies.notes: City operates 4 ambulances within Clayton County.

agencies.name: City of Morrow Fire and EMS
agencies.notes: City operates 3 ambulances within Clayton County.

Barrow.primary_provider: Barrow County Fire and EMS / NE Georgia Physicians Group

Bibb.primary_provider: Atrium Health Navicent EMS / Community Ambulance (dual zone)

Chattooga.primary_provider: Atrium Health Floyd EMS / Redmond Regional EMS

Cobb.primary_provider: MetroAtlanta Ambulance Service / Puckett EMS (dual zone)

Fayette.primary_provider: Fayette County Fire & Emergency Services / AMR Coweta

Muscogee.primary_provider: Columbus Fire & EMS / EMS Care Ambulance / Community Ambulance (multi-provider)

Pulaski.primary_provider: Heartland EMS / Taylor Regional Hospital EMS

Taliaferro.primary_provider: Unknown - smallest county in Georgia by population

athens_area_detail.description: Athens-Clarke County and surrounding counties in northeast Georgia (EMS Region 10). National EMS (Priority Ambulance) is the dominant private 911 provider in the immediate Athens area.

Clarke.notes: Contracted 911 provider for Athens-Clarke County. CAAS accredited since 1983. UGA Athletic Association preferred provider.

Oconee.notes: National EMS holds 911 contract for Oconee County.

Morgan.notes: National EMS partners with Morgan County for 911 ambulance service.

Oglethorpe.notes: County-operated EMS. Small rural county east of Athens.

Jackson.notes: County-run EMS. 7 ALS ambulances. 95%+ paramedic staffing. Northwest of Athens.

Barrow.primary_provider: Barrow County Fire and EMS / NE Georgia Physicians Group
Barrow.notes: Fire-based EMS with public-private partnership through NGMC/Northeast Georgia Physicians Group. West of Athens.

Walton.notes: County-run EMS. 6 ambulances 24/7 + 2 reserve. Southwest of Athens.

Greene.notes: County-run EMS. 3 stations. Southeast of Athens.

Elbert.notes: County-run EMS. East of Athens near SC border.

major_private_providers: Priority Ambulance (Central EMS, National EMS, Puckett EMS, Ambucare, Angel EMS) - ~20 counties
summary_stats.notes: Ambulance counts from FY2021 GTCNC grant. Some counties have multiple providers operating in zones or with mutual aid agreements. Grady EMS is the single largest fleet operator (~150 ambulances across all service areas).
